17#include "apr_lib.h"

42#include "cache_socache_common.h"
43
44/*
45 * mod_cache_socache: Shared Object Cache Based HTTP 1.1 Cache.
46 *
47 * Flow to Find the entry:
48 *   Incoming client requests URI /foo/bar/baz
49 *   Fetch URI key (may contain Format #1 or Format #2)
50 *   If format #1 (Contains a list of Vary Headers):
51 *      Use each header name (from .header) with our request values (headers_in) to
52 *      regenerate key using HeaderName+HeaderValue+.../foo/bar/baz
53 *      re-read in key (must be format #2)
54 *
55 * Format #1:
56 *   apr_uint32_t format;
57 *   apr_time_t expire;
58 *   apr_array_t vary_headers (delimited by CRLF)
59 *
60 * Format #2:
61 *   cache_socache_info_t (first sizeof(apr_uint32_t) bytes is the format)
62 *   entity name (sobj->name) [length is in cache_socache_info_t->name_len]
63 *   r->headers_out (delimited by CRLF)
64 *   CRLF
65 *   r->headers_in (delimited by CRLF)
66 *   CRLF
67 */ view japanese
68
69module AP_MODULE_DECLARE_DATA cache_socache_module;
70
71/*
72 * cache_socache_object_t
73 * Pointed to by cache_object_t::vobj
74 */ view japanese
75typedef struct cache_socache_object_t
76{
77    apr_pool_t *pool; /* pool */ view japanese
78    unsigned char *buffer; /* the cache buffer */ view japanese
79    apr_size_t buffer_len; /* size of the buffer */ view japanese
80    apr_bucket_brigade *body; /* brigade containing the body, if any */ view japanese
81    apr_table_t *headers_in; /* Input headers to save */ view japanese
82    apr_table_t *headers_out; /* Output headers to save */ view japanese
83    cache_socache_info_t socache_info; /* Header information. */ view japanese
84    apr_size_t body_offset; /* offset to the start of the body */ view japanese
85    apr_off_t body_length; /* length of the cached entity body */ view japanese
86    apr_time_t expire; /* when to expire the entry */ view japanese
87
88    const char *name; /* Requested URI without vary bits - suitable for mortals. */ view japanese
89    const char *key; /* On-disk prefix; URI with Vary bits (if present) */ view japanese
90    apr_off_t offset; /* Max size to set aside */ view japanese
91    apr_time_t timeout; /* Max time to set aside */ view japanese
92    unsigned int newbody :1; /* whether a new body is present */ view japanese
93    unsigned int done :1; /* Is the attempt to cache complete? */ view japanese
94} cache_socache_object_t;
95
96/*
97 * mod_cache_socache configuration
98 */ view japanese
99#define DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_SIZE 100*1024
100#define DEFAULT_MAXTIME 86400
101#define DEFAULT_MINTIME 600
102#define DEFAULT_READSIZE 0
103#define DEFAULT_READTIME 0
104
105typedef struct cache_socache_provider_conf
106{
107    const char *args;
108    ap_socache_provider_t *socache_provider;
109    ap_socache_instance_t *socache_instance;
110} cache_socache_provider_conf;
111
112typedef struct cache_socache_conf
113{
114    cache_socache_provider_conf *provider;
115} cache_socache_conf;
116
117typedef struct cache_socache_dir_conf
118{
119    apr_off_t max; /* maximum file size for cached files */ view japanese
120    apr_time_t maxtime; /* maximum expiry time */ view japanese
121    apr_time_t mintime; /* minimum expiry time */ view japanese
122    apr_off_t readsize; /* maximum data to attempt to cache in one go */ view japanese
123    apr_time_t readtime; /* maximum time taken to cache in one go */ view japanese
124    unsigned int max_set :1;
125    unsigned int maxtime_set :1;
126    unsigned int mintime_set :1;
127    unsigned int readsize_set :1;
128    unsigned int readtime_set :1;
129} cache_socache_dir_conf;
130
131/* Shared object cache and mutex */ view japanese
132static const char * const cache_socache_id = "cache-socache";
133static apr_global_mutex_t *socache_mutex = NULL;
134
135/*
136 * Local static functions
137 */ view japanese
138
139static apr_status_t read_array(request_rec *r, apr_array_header_t *arr,
140        unsigned char *buffer, apr_size_t buffer_len, apr_size_t *slider)
141{
142    apr_size_t val = *slider;
143
144    while (*slider < buffer_len) {
145        if (buffer[*slider] == '\r') {
146            if (val == *slider) {
147                (*slider)++;
148                return APR_SUCCESS;
149            }
150            *((const char **) apr_array_push(arr)) = apr_pstrndup(r->pool,
151                    (const char *) buffer + val, *slider - val);
152            (*slider)++;
153            if (buffer[*slider] == '\n') {
154                (*slider)++;
155            }
156            val = *slider;
157        }
158        else if (buffer[*slider] == '\0') {
159            (*slider)++;
160            return APR_SUCCESS;
161        }
162        else {
163            (*slider)++;
164        }
165    }
166
167    return APR_EOF;
168}
169
170static apr_status_t store_array(apr_array_header_t *arr, unsigned char *buffer,
171        apr_size_t buffer_len, apr_size_t *slider)
172{
173    int i, len;
174    const char **elts;
175
176    elts = (const char **) arr->elts;
177
178    for (i = 0; i < arr->nelts; i++) {
179        apr_size_t e_len = strlen(elts[i]);
180        if (e_len + 3 >= buffer_len - *slider) {
181            return APR_EOF;
182        }
183        len = apr_snprintf(buffer ? (char *) buffer + *slider : NULL,
184                buffer ? buffer_len - *slider : 0, "%s" CRLF, elts[i]);
185        *slider += len;
186    }
187    if (buffer) {
188        memcpy(buffer + *slider, CRLF, sizeof(CRLF) - 1);
189    }
190    *slider += sizeof(CRLF) - 1;
191
192    return APR_SUCCESS;
193}
194
195static apr_status_t read_table(cache_handle_t *handle, request_rec *r,
196        apr_table_t *table, unsigned char *buffer, apr_size_t buffer_len,
197        apr_size_t *slider)
198{
199    apr_size_t key = *slider, colon = 0, len = 0;
200
201    while (*slider < buffer_len) {
202        if (buffer[*slider] == ':') {
203            if (!colon) {
204                colon = *slider;
205            }
206            (*slider)++;
207        }
208        else if (buffer[*slider] == '\r') {
209            len = colon;
210            if (key == *slider) {
211                (*slider)++;
212                if (buffer[*slider] == '\n') {
213                    (*slider)++;
214                }
215                return APR_SUCCESS;
216            }
217            if (!colon || buffer[colon++] != ':') {
218                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(02344)
219                        "Premature end of cache headers.");
220                return APR_EGENERAL;
221            }
222            /* Do not go past the \r from above as apr_isspace('\r') is true */ view japanese
223            while (apr_isspace(buffer[colon]) && (colon < *slider)) {
224                colon++;
225            }
226            apr_table_addn(table, apr_pstrmemdup(r->pool, (const char *) buffer
227                    + key, len - key), apr_pstrmemdup(r->pool,
228                    (const char *) buffer + colon, *slider - colon));
229            (*slider)++;
230            if (buffer[*slider] == '\n') {
231                (*slider)++;
232            }
233            key = *slider;
234            colon = 0;
235        }
236        else if (buffer[*slider] == '\0') {
237            (*slider)++;
238            return APR_SUCCESS;
239        }
240        else {
241            (*slider)++;
242        }
243    }
244
245    return APR_EOF;
246}
247
248static apr_status_t store_table(apr_table_t *table, unsigned char *buffer,
249        apr_size_t buffer_len, apr_size_t *slider)
250{
251    int i, len;
252    apr_table_entry_t *elts;
253
254    elts = (apr_table_entry_t *) apr_table_elts(table)->elts;
255    for (i = 0; i < apr_table_elts(table)->nelts; ++i) {
256        if (elts[i].key != NULL) {
257            apr_size_t key_len = strlen(elts[i].key);
258            apr_size_t val_len = strlen(elts[i].val);
259            if (key_len + val_len + 5 >= buffer_len - *slider) {
260                return APR_EOF;
261            }
262            len = apr_snprintf(buffer ? (char *) buffer + *slider : NULL,
263                    buffer ? buffer_len - *slider : 0, "%s: %s" CRLF,
264                    elts[i].key, elts[i].val);
265            *slider += len;
266        }
267    }
268    if (3 >= buffer_len - *slider) {
269        return APR_EOF;
270    }
271    if (buffer) {
272        memcpy(buffer + *slider, CRLF, sizeof(CRLF) - 1);
273    }
274    *slider += sizeof(CRLF) - 1;
275
276    return APR_SUCCESS;
277}
278
279static const char* regen_key(apr_pool_t *p, apr_table_t *headers,
280                             apr_array_header_t *varray, const char *oldkey,
281                             apr_size_t *newkeylen)
282{
283    struct iovec *iov;
284    int i, k;
285    int nvec;
286    const char *header;
287    const char **elts;
288
289    nvec = (varray->nelts * 2) + 1;
290    iov = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(struct iovec) * nvec);
291    elts = (const char **) varray->elts;
292
293    /* TODO:
294     *    - Handle multiple-value headers better. (sort them?)
295     *    - Handle Case in-sensitive Values better.
296     *        This isn't the end of the world, since it just lowers the cache
297     *        hit rate, but it would be nice to fix.
298     *
299     * The majority are case insenstive if they are values (encoding etc).
300     * Most of rfc2616 is case insensitive on header contents.
301     *
302     * So the better solution may be to identify headers which should be
303     * treated case-sensitive?
304     *  HTTP URI's (3.2.3) [host and scheme are insensitive]
305     *  HTTP method (5.1.1)
306     *  HTTP-date values (3.3.1)
307     *  3.7 Media Types [excerpt]
308     *     The type, subtype, and parameter attribute names are case-
309     *     insensitive. Parameter values might or might not be case-sensitive,
310     *     depending on the semantics of the parameter name.
311     *  4.20 Except [excerpt]
312     *     Comparison of expectation values is case-insensitive for unquoted
313     *     tokens (including the 100-continue token), and is case-sensitive for
314     *     quoted-string expectation-extensions.
315     */ view japanese
316
317    for (i = 0, k = 0; i < varray->nelts; i++) {
318        header = apr_table_get(headers, elts[i]);
319        if (!header) {
320            header = "";
321        }
322        iov[k].iov_base = (char*) elts[i];
323        iov[k].iov_len = strlen(elts[i]);
324        k++;
325        iov[k].iov_base = (char*) header;
326        iov[k].iov_len = strlen(header);
327        k++;
328    }
329    iov[k].iov_base = (char*) oldkey;
330    iov[k].iov_len = strlen(oldkey);
331    k++;
332
333    return apr_pstrcatv(p, iov, k, newkeylen);
334}
335
336static int array_alphasort(const void *fn1, const void *fn2)
337{
338    return strcmp(*(char**) fn1, *(char**) fn2);
339}
340
341static void tokens_to_array(apr_pool_t *p, const char *data,
342        apr_array_header_t *arr)
343{
344    char *token;
345
346    while ((token = ap_get_list_item(p, &data)) != NULL) {
347        *((const char **) apr_array_push(arr)) = token;
348    }
349
350    /* Sort it so that "Vary: A, B" and "Vary: B, A" are stored the same. */ view japanese
351    qsort((void *) arr->elts, arr->nelts, sizeof(char *), array_alphasort);
352}
353
354/*
355 * Hook and mod_cache callback functions
356 */ view japanese
357static int create_entity(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r, const char *key,
358        apr_off_t len, apr_bucket_brigade *bb)
359{
360    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf =
361            ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &cache_socache_module);
362    cache_socache_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
363            &cache_socache_module);
364    cache_object_t *obj;
365    cache_socache_object_t *sobj;
366    apr_size_t total;
367
368    if (conf->provider == NULL) {
369        return DECLINED;
370    }
371
372    /* we don't support caching of range requests (yet) */ view japanese
373    /* TODO: but we could */ view japanese
374    if (r->status == HTTP_PARTIAL_CONTENT) {
375        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02345)
376                "URL %s partial content response not cached",
377                key);
378        return DECLINED;
379    }
380
381    /*
382     * We have a chicken and egg problem. We don't know until we
383     * attempt to store_headers just how big the response will be
384     * and whether it will fit in the cache limits set. But we
385     * need to make a decision now as to whether we plan to try.
386     * If we make the wrong decision, we could prevent another
387     * cache implementation, such as cache_disk, from getting the
388     * opportunity to cache, and that would be unfortunate.
389     *
390     * In a series of tests, from cheapest to most expensive,
391     * decide whether or not to ignore this attempt to cache,
392     * with a small margin just to be sure.
393     */ view japanese
394    if (len < 0) {
395        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02346)
396                "URL '%s' had no explicit size, ignoring", key);
397        return DECLINED;
398    }
399    if (len > dconf->max) {
400        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02347)
401                "URL '%s' body larger than limit, ignoring "
402                "(%" APR_OFF_T_FMT " > %" APR_OFF_T_FMT ")",
403                key, len, dconf->max);
404        return DECLINED;
405    }
406
407    /* estimate the total cached size, given current headers */ view japanese
408    total = len + sizeof(cache_socache_info_t) + strlen(key);
409    if (APR_SUCCESS != store_table(r->headers_out, NULL, dconf->max, &total)
410            || APR_SUCCESS != store_table(r->headers_in, NULL, dconf->max,
411                    &total)) {
412        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02348)
413                "URL '%s' estimated headers size larger than limit, ignoring "
414                "(%" APR_SIZE_T_FMT " > %" APR_OFF_T_FMT ")",
415                key, total, dconf->max);
416        return DECLINED;
417    }
418
419    if (total >= dconf->max) {
420        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02349)
421                "URL '%s' body and headers larger than limit, ignoring "
422                "(%" APR_OFF_T_FMT " > %" APR_OFF_T_FMT ")",
423                key, len, dconf->max);
424        return DECLINED;
425    }
426
427    /* Allocate and initialize cache_object_t and cache_socache_object_t */ view japanese
428    h->cache_obj = obj = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(*obj));
429    obj->vobj = sobj = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(*sobj));
430
431    obj->key = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, key);
432    sobj->key = obj->key;
433    sobj->name = obj->key;
434
435    return OK;
436}
437
438static apr_status_t sobj_body_pre_cleanup(void *baton)
439{
440    cache_socache_object_t *sobj = baton;
441    apr_brigade_cleanup(sobj->body);
442    sobj->body = NULL;
443    return APR_SUCCESS;
444}
445
446static int open_entity(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r, const char *key)
447{
448    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf =
449            ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &cache_socache_module);
450    cache_socache_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
451            &cache_socache_module);
452    apr_uint32_t format;
453    apr_size_t slider;
454    unsigned int buffer_len;
455    const char *nkey;
456    apr_status_t rc;
457    cache_object_t *obj;
458    cache_info *info;
459    cache_socache_object_t *sobj;
460    apr_size_t len;
461
462    nkey = NULL;
463    h->cache_obj = NULL;
464
465    if (!conf->provider || !conf->provider->socache_instance) {
466        return DECLINED;
467    }
468
469    /* Create and init the cache object */ view japanese
470    obj = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(cache_object_t));
471    sobj = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(cache_socache_object_t));
472
473    info = &(obj->info);
474
475    /* Create a temporary pool for the buffer, and destroy it if something
476     * goes wrong so we don't have large buffers of unused memory hanging
477     * about for the lifetime of the response.
478     */ view japanese
479    apr_pool_create(&sobj->pool, r->pool);
480    apr_pool_tag(sobj->pool, "mod_cache_socache (open_entity)");
481
482    sobj->buffer = apr_palloc(sobj->pool, dconf->max);
483    sobj->buffer_len = dconf->max;
484
485    /* attempt to retrieve the cached entry */
486    if (socache_mutex) {
487        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
488        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
489            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02350)
490                    "could not acquire lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
491            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
492            sobj->pool = NULL;
493            return DECLINED;
494        }
495    }
496    buffer_len = sobj->buffer_len;
497    rc = conf->provider->socache_provider->retrieve(
498            conf->provider->socache_instance, r->server, (unsigned char *) key,
499            strlen(key), sobj->buffer, &buffer_len, r->pool);
500    if (socache_mutex) {
501        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
502        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
503            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02351)
504                    "could not release lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
505            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
506            sobj->pool = NULL;
507            return DECLINED;
508        }
509    }
510    if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
511        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rc, r, APLOGNO(02352)
512                "Key not found in cache: %s", key);
513        apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
514        sobj->pool = NULL;
515        return DECLINED;
516    }
517    if (buffer_len >= sobj->buffer_len) {
518        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rc, r, APLOGNO(02353)
519                "Key found in cache but too big, ignoring: %s", key);
520        apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
521        sobj->pool = NULL;
522        return DECLINED;
523    }
524
525    /* read the format from the cache file */ view japanese
526    memcpy(&format, sobj->buffer, sizeof(format));
527    slider = sizeof(format);
528
529    if (format == CACHE_SOCACHE_VARY_FORMAT_VERSION) {
530        apr_array_header_t* varray;
531        apr_time_t expire;
532
533        memcpy(&expire, sobj->buffer + slider, sizeof(expire));
534        slider += sizeof(expire);
535
536        varray = apr_array_make(r->pool, 5, sizeof(char*));
537        rc = read_array(r, varray, sobj->buffer, buffer_len, &slider);
538        if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
539            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, r, APLOGNO(02354)
540                    "Cannot parse vary entry for key: %s", key);
541            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
542            sobj->pool = NULL;
543            return DECLINED;
544        }
545
546        nkey = regen_key(r->pool, r->headers_in, varray, key, &len);
547
548        /* attempt to retrieve the cached entry */ view japanese
549        if (socache_mutex) {
550            apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
551            if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
552                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02355)
553                        "could not acquire lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
554                apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
555                sobj->pool = NULL;
556                return DECLINED;
557            }
558        }
559        buffer_len = sobj->buffer_len;
560        rc = conf->provider->socache_provider->retrieve(
561                conf->provider->socache_instance, r->server,
562                (unsigned char *) nkey, len, sobj->buffer,
563                &buffer_len, r->pool);
564        if (socache_mutex) {
565            apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
566            if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
567                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02356)
568                        "could not release lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
569                apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
570                sobj->pool = NULL;
571                return DECLINED;
572            }
573        }
574        if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
575            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rc, r, APLOGNO(02357)
576                    "Key not found in cache: %s", key);
577            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
578            sobj->pool = NULL;
579            return DECLINED;
580        }
581        if (buffer_len >= sobj->buffer_len) {
582            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rc, r, APLOGNO(02358)
583                    "Key found in cache but too big, ignoring: %s", key);
584            goto fail;
585        }
586
587    }
588    else if (format != CACHE_SOCACHE_DISK_FORMAT_VERSION) {
589        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(02359)
590                "Key '%s' found in cache has version %d, expected %d, ignoring",
591                key, format, CACHE_SOCACHE_DISK_FORMAT_VERSION);
592        goto fail;
593    }
594    else {
595        nkey = key;
596    }
597
598    obj->key = nkey;
599    sobj->key = nkey;
600    sobj->name = key;
601
602    if (buffer_len >= sizeof(cache_socache_info_t)) {
603        memcpy(&sobj->socache_info, sobj->buffer, sizeof(cache_socache_info_t));
604    }
605    else {
606        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, r, APLOGNO(02360)
607                "Cache entry for key '%s' too short, removing", nkey);
608        goto fail;
609    }
610    slider = sizeof(cache_socache_info_t);
611
612    /* Store it away so we can get it later. */ view japanese
613    info->status = sobj->socache_info.status;
614    info->date = sobj->socache_info.date;
615    info->expire = sobj->socache_info.expire;
616    info->request_time = sobj->socache_info.request_time;
617    info->response_time = sobj->socache_info.response_time;
618
619    memcpy(&info->control, &sobj->socache_info.control, sizeof(cache_control_t));
620
621    if (sobj->socache_info.name_len <= buffer_len - slider) {
622        if (strncmp((const char *) sobj->buffer + slider, sobj->name,
623                sobj->socache_info.name_len)) {
624            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, r, APLOGNO(02361)
625                    "Cache entry for key '%s' URL mismatch, ignoring", nkey);
626            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
627            sobj->pool = NULL;
628            return DECLINED;
629        }
630        slider += sobj->socache_info.name_len;
631    }
632    else {
633        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, r, APLOGNO(02362)
634                "Cache entry for key '%s' too short, removing", nkey);
635        goto fail;
636    }
637
638    /* Is this a cached HEAD request? */ view japanese
639    if (sobj->socache_info.header_only && !r->header_only) {
640        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, APR_SUCCESS, r, APLOGNO(02363)
641                "HEAD request cached, non-HEAD requested, ignoring: %s",
642                sobj->key);
643        apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
644        sobj->pool = NULL;
645        return DECLINED;
646    }
647
648    h->req_hdrs = apr_table_make(r->pool, 20);
649    h->resp_hdrs = apr_table_make(r->pool, 20);
650
651    /* Call routine to read the header lines/status line */ view japanese
652    if (APR_SUCCESS != read_table(h, r, h->resp_hdrs, sobj->buffer, buffer_len,
653            &slider)) {
654        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, r, APLOGNO(02364)
655                "Cache entry for key '%s' response headers unreadable, removing", nkey);
656        goto fail;
657    }
658    if (APR_SUCCESS != read_table(h, r, h->req_hdrs, sobj->buffer, buffer_len,
659            &slider)) {
660        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, r, APLOGNO(02365)
661                "Cache entry for key '%s' request headers unreadable, removing", nkey);
662        goto fail;
663    }
664
665    /* Retrieve the body if we have one */ view japanese
666    len = buffer_len - slider;
667    if (len > 0) {
668        apr_bucket *e;
669        /* Create the body brigade later concatenated to the output filters'
670         * brigade by recall_body(). Since sobj->buffer (the data) point to
671         * sobj->pool (a subpool of r->pool), be safe by using a pool bucket
672         * which can morph to heap if sobj->pool is destroyed while the bucket
673         * is still alive. But if sobj->pool gets destroyed while the bucket is
674         * still in sobj->body (i.e. recall_body() was never called), we don't
675         * need to morph to something just about to be freed, so a pre_cleanup
676         * will take care of cleaning up sobj->body before this happens (and is
677         * a noop otherwise).
678         */ view japanese
679        sobj->body = apr_brigade_create(sobj->pool, r->connection->bucket_alloc);
680        apr_pool_pre_cleanup_register(sobj->pool, sobj, sobj_body_pre_cleanup);
681        e = apr_bucket_pool_create((const char *) sobj->buffer + slider, len,
682                                   sobj->pool, r->connection->bucket_alloc);
683        APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(sobj->body, e);
684    }
685
686    /* make the configuration stick */ view japanese
687    h->cache_obj = obj;
688    obj->vobj = sobj;
689
690    return OK;
691
692fail:
693    if (socache_mutex) {
694        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
695        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
696            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02366)
697                    "could not acquire lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
698            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
699            sobj->pool = NULL;
700            return DECLINED;
701        }
702    }
703    if (nkey) {
704        conf->provider->socache_provider->remove(
705                conf->provider->socache_instance, r->server,
706                (unsigned char *) nkey, strlen(nkey), r->pool);
707    }
708    if (socache_mutex) {
709        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
710        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
711            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02367)
712                    "could not release lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
713        }
714    }
715    apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
716    sobj->pool = NULL;
717    return DECLINED;
718}
719
720static int remove_entity(cache_handle_t *h)
721{
722    /* Null out the cache object pointer so next time we start from scratch  */ view japanese
723    h->cache_obj = NULL;
724    return OK;
725}
726
727static int remove_url(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r)
728{
729    cache_socache_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
730            &cache_socache_module);
731    cache_socache_object_t *sobj;
732
733    sobj = (cache_socache_object_t *) h->cache_obj->vobj;
734    if (!sobj) {
735        return DECLINED;
736    }
737
738    /* Remove the key from the cache */ view japanese
739    if (socache_mutex) {
740        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
741        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
742            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02368)
743                    "could not acquire lock, ignoring: %s", sobj->key);
744            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
745            sobj->pool = NULL;
746            return DECLINED;
747        }
748    }
749    conf->provider->socache_provider->remove(conf->provider->socache_instance,
750            r->server, (unsigned char *) sobj->key, strlen(sobj->key), r->pool);
751    if (socache_mutex) {
752        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
753        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
754            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02369)
755                    "could not release lock, ignoring: %s", sobj->key);
756            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
757            sobj->pool = NULL;
758            return DECLINED;
759        }
760    }
761
762    return OK;
763}
764
765static apr_status_t recall_headers(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r)
766{
767    /* we recalled the headers during open_entity, so do nothing */ view japanese
768    return APR_SUCCESS;
769}
770
771static apr_status_t recall_body(cache_handle_t *h, apr_pool_t *p,
772        apr_bucket_brigade *bb)
773{
774    cache_socache_object_t *sobj = (cache_socache_object_t*) h->cache_obj->vobj;
775
776    if (sobj->body) {
777        APR_BRIGADE_CONCAT(bb, sobj->body);
778    }
779
780    return APR_SUCCESS;
781}
782
783static apr_status_t store_headers(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r,
784        cache_info *info)
785{
786    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf =
787            ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &cache_socache_module);
788    cache_socache_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
789            &cache_socache_module);
790    apr_size_t slider;
791    apr_status_t rv;
792    cache_object_t *obj = h->cache_obj;
793    cache_socache_object_t *sobj = (cache_socache_object_t*) obj->vobj;
794    cache_socache_info_t *socache_info;
795
796    memcpy(&h->cache_obj->info, info, sizeof(cache_info));
797
798    if (r->headers_out) {
799        sobj->headers_out = ap_cache_cacheable_headers_out(r);
800    }
801
802    if (r->headers_in) {
803        sobj->headers_in = ap_cache_cacheable_headers_in(r);
804    }
805
806    sobj->expire
807            = obj->info.expire > r->request_time + dconf->maxtime ? r->request_time
808                    + dconf->maxtime
809                    : obj->info.expire + dconf->mintime;
810
811    apr_pool_create(&sobj->pool, r->pool);
812    apr_pool_tag(sobj->pool, "mod_cache_socache (store_headers)");
813
814    sobj->buffer = apr_palloc(sobj->pool, dconf->max);
815    sobj->buffer_len = dconf->max;
816    socache_info = (cache_socache_info_t *) sobj->buffer;
817
818    if (sobj->headers_out) {
819        const char *vary;
820
821        vary = apr_table_get(sobj->headers_out, "Vary");
822
823        if (vary) {
824            apr_array_header_t* varray;
825            apr_uint32_t format = CACHE_SOCACHE_VARY_FORMAT_VERSION;
826
827            memcpy(sobj->buffer, &format, sizeof(format));
828            slider = sizeof(format);
829
830            memcpy(sobj->buffer + slider, &obj->info.expire,
831                    sizeof(obj->info.expire));
832            slider += sizeof(obj->info.expire);
833
834            varray = apr_array_make(r->pool, 6, sizeof(char*));
835            tokens_to_array(r->pool, vary, varray);
836
837            if (APR_SUCCESS != (rv = store_array(varray, sobj->buffer,
838                    sobj->buffer_len, &slider))) {
839                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, r, APLOGNO(02370)
840                        "buffer too small for Vary array, caching aborted: %s",
841                        obj->key);
842                apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
843                sobj->pool = NULL;
844                return rv;
845            }
846            if (socache_mutex) {
847                apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
848                if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
849                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02371)
850                            "could not acquire lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
851                    apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
852                    sobj->pool = NULL;
853                    return status;
854                }
855            }
856            rv = conf->provider->socache_provider->store(
857                    conf->provider->socache_instance, r->server,
858                    (unsigned char *) obj->key, strlen(obj->key), sobj->expire,
859                    (unsigned char *) sobj->buffer, (unsigned int) slider,
860                    sobj->pool);
861            if (socache_mutex) {
862                apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
863                if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
864                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02372)
865                            "could not release lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
866                }
867            }
868            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
869                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, r, APLOGNO(02373)
870                        "Vary not written to cache, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
871                apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
872                sobj->pool = NULL;
873                return rv;
874            }
875
876            obj->key = sobj->key = regen_key(r->pool, sobj->headers_in, varray,
877                                             sobj->name, NULL);
878        }
879    }
880
881    socache_info->format = CACHE_SOCACHE_DISK_FORMAT_VERSION;
882    socache_info->date = obj->info.date;
883    socache_info->expire = obj->info.expire;
884    socache_info->entity_version = sobj->socache_info.entity_version++;
885    socache_info->request_time = obj->info.request_time;
886    socache_info->response_time = obj->info.response_time;
887    socache_info->status = obj->info.status;
888
889    if (r->header_only && r->status != HTTP_NOT_MODIFIED) {
890        socache_info->header_only = 1;
891    }
892    else {
893        socache_info->header_only = sobj->socache_info.header_only;
894    }
895
896    socache_info->name_len = strlen(sobj->name);
897
898    memcpy(&socache_info->control, &obj->info.control, sizeof(cache_control_t));
899    slider = sizeof(cache_socache_info_t);
900
901    if (slider + socache_info->name_len >= sobj->buffer_len) {
902        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, r, APLOGNO(02374)
903                "cache buffer too small for name: %s",
904                sobj->name);
905        apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
906        sobj->pool = NULL;
907        return APR_EGENERAL;
908    }
909    memcpy(sobj->buffer + slider, sobj->name, socache_info->name_len);
910    slider += socache_info->name_len;
911
912    if (sobj->headers_out) {
913        if (APR_SUCCESS != store_table(sobj->headers_out, sobj->buffer,
914                sobj->buffer_len, &slider)) {
915            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, r, APLOGNO(02375)
916                    "out-headers didn't fit in buffer: %s", sobj->name);
917            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
918            sobj->pool = NULL;
919            return APR_EGENERAL;
920        }
921    }
922
923    /* Parse the vary header and dump those fields from the headers_in. */ view japanese
924    /* TODO: Make call to the same thing cache_select calls to crack Vary. */ view japanese
925    if (sobj->headers_in) {
926        if (APR_SUCCESS != store_table(sobj->headers_in, sobj->buffer,
927                sobj->buffer_len, &slider)) {
928            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, r, APLOGNO(02376)
929                    "in-headers didn't fit in buffer %s",
930                    sobj->key);
931            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
932            sobj->pool = NULL;
933            return APR_EGENERAL;
934        }
935    }
936
937    sobj->body_offset = slider;
938
939    return APR_SUCCESS;
940}
941
942static apr_status_t store_body(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r,
943        apr_bucket_brigade *in, apr_bucket_brigade *out)
944{
945    apr_bucket *e;
946    apr_status_t rv = APR_SUCCESS;
947    cache_socache_object_t *sobj =
948            (cache_socache_object_t *) h->cache_obj->vobj;
949    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf =
950            ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &cache_socache_module);
951    int seen_eos = 0;
952
953    if (!sobj->offset) {
954        sobj->offset = dconf->readsize;
955    }
956    if (!sobj->timeout && dconf->readtime) {
957        sobj->timeout = apr_time_now() + dconf->readtime;
958    }
959
960    if (!sobj->newbody) {
961        sobj->body_length = 0;
962        sobj->newbody = 1;
963    }
964    if (sobj->offset) {
965        apr_brigade_partition(in, sobj->offset, &e);
966    }
967
968    while (APR_SUCCESS == rv && !APR_BRIGADE_EMPTY(in)) {
969        const char *str;
970        apr_size_t length;
971
972        e = APR_BRIGADE_FIRST(in);
973
974        /* are we done completely? if so, pass any trailing buckets right through */ view japanese
975        if (sobj->done || !sobj->pool) {
976            APR_BUCKET_REMOVE(e);
977            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(out, e);
978            continue;
979        }
980
981        /* have we seen eos yet? */ view japanese
982        if (APR_BUCKET_IS_EOS(e)) {
983            seen_eos = 1;
984            sobj->done = 1;
985            APR_BUCKET_REMOVE(e);
986            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(out, e);
987            break;
988        }
989
990        /* honour flush buckets, we'll get called again */ view japanese
991        if (APR_BUCKET_IS_FLUSH(e)) {
992            APR_BUCKET_REMOVE(e);
993            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(out, e);
994            break;
995        }
996
997        /* metadata buckets are preserved as is */ view japanese
998        if (APR_BUCKET_IS_METADATA(e)) {
999            APR_BUCKET_REMOVE(e);
1000            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(out, e);
1001            continue;
1002        }
1003
1004        /* read the bucket, write to the cache */ view japanese
1005        rv = apr_bucket_read(e, &str, &length, APR_BLOCK_READ);
1006        APR_BUCKET_REMOVE(e);
1007        APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(out, e);
1008        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1009            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(02377)
1010                    "Error when reading bucket for URL %s",
1011                    h->cache_obj->key);
1012            /* Remove the intermediate cache file and return non-APR_SUCCESS */ view japanese
1013            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1014            sobj->pool = NULL;
1015            return rv;
1016        }
1017
1018        /* don't write empty buckets to the cache */ view japanese
1019        if (!length) {
1020            continue;
1021        }
1022
1023        sobj->body_length += length;
1024        if (sobj->body_length >= sobj->buffer_len - sobj->body_offset) {
1025            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02378)
1026                    "URL %s failed the buffer size check "
1027                    "(%" APR_OFF_T_FMT ">=%" APR_SIZE_T_FMT ")",
1028                    h->cache_obj->key, sobj->body_length,
1029                    sobj->buffer_len - sobj->body_offset);
1030            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1031            sobj->pool = NULL;
1032            return APR_EGENERAL;
1033        }
1034        memcpy(sobj->buffer + sobj->body_offset + sobj->body_length - length,
1035               str, length);
1036
1037        /* have we reached the limit of how much we're prepared to write in one
1038         * go? If so, leave, we'll get called again. This prevents us from trying
1039         * to swallow too much data at once, or taking so long to write the data
1040         * the client times out.
1041         */ view japanese
1042        sobj->offset -= length;
1043        if (sobj->offset <= 0) {
1044            sobj->offset = 0;
1045            break;
1046        }
1047        if ((dconf->readtime && apr_time_now() > sobj->timeout)) {
1048            sobj->timeout = 0;
1049            break;
1050        }
1051
1052    }
1053
1054    /* Was this the final bucket? If yes, perform sanity checks.
1055     */ view japanese
1056    if (seen_eos) {
1057        const char *cl_header;
1058        apr_off_t cl;
1059
1060        if (r->connection->aborted || r->no_cache) {
1061            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, r, APLOGNO(02380)
1062                    "Discarding body for URL %s "
1063                    "because connection has been aborted.",
1064                    h->cache_obj->key);
1065            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1066            sobj->pool = NULL;
1067            return APR_EGENERAL;
1068        }
1069
1070        cl_header = apr_table_get(r->headers_out, "Content-Length");
1071        if (cl_header && (!ap_parse_strict_length(&cl, cl_header)
1072                          || cl != sobj->body_length)) {
1073            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02381)
1074                    "URL %s didn't receive complete response, not caching",
1075                    h->cache_obj->key);
1076            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1077            sobj->pool = NULL;
1078            return APR_EGENERAL;
1079        }
1080
1081        /* All checks were fine, we're good to go when the commit comes */ view japanese
1082
1083    }
1084
1085    return APR_SUCCESS;
1086}
1087
1088static apr_status_t commit_entity(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r)
1089{
1090    cache_socache_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
1091            &cache_socache_module);
1092    cache_object_t *obj = h->cache_obj;
1093    cache_socache_object_t *sobj = (cache_socache_object_t *) obj->vobj;
1094    apr_status_t rv;
1095
1096    if (socache_mutex) {
1097        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
1098        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
1099            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02384)
1100                    "could not acquire lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
1101            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1102            sobj->pool = NULL;
1103            return status;
1104        }
1105    }
1106    rv = conf->provider->socache_provider->store(
1107            conf->provider->socache_instance, r->server,
1108            (unsigned char *) sobj->key, strlen(sobj->key), sobj->expire,
1109            sobj->buffer, sobj->body_offset + sobj->body_length, sobj->pool);
1110    if (socache_mutex) {
1111        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
1112        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
1113            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02385)
1114                    "could not release lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
1115            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1116            sobj->pool = NULL;
1117            return status;
1118        }
1119    }
1120    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1121        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, rv, r, APLOGNO(02386)
1122                "could not write to cache, ignoring: %s", sobj->key);
1123        goto fail;
1124    }
1125
1126    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02387)
1127            "commit_entity: Headers and body for URL %s cached for maximum of %d seconds.",
1128            sobj->name, (apr_uint32_t)apr_time_sec(sobj->expire - r->request_time));
1129
1130    apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1131    sobj->pool = NULL;
1132
1133    return APR_SUCCESS;
1134
1135fail:
1136    /* For safety, remove any existing entry on failure, just in case it could not
1137     * be revalidated successfully.
1138     */ view japanese
1139    if (socache_mutex) {
1140        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
1141        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
1142            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02388)
1143                    "could not acquire lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
1144            apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1145            sobj->pool = NULL;
1146            return rv;
1147        }
1148    }
1149    conf->provider->socache_provider->remove(conf->provider->socache_instance,
1150            r->server, (unsigned char *) sobj->key, strlen(sobj->key), r->pool);
1151    if (socache_mutex) {
1152        apr_status_t status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
1153        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
1154            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02389)
1155                    "could not release lock, ignoring: %s", obj->key);
1156        }
1157    }
1158
1159    apr_pool_destroy(sobj->pool);
1160    sobj->pool = NULL;
1161    return rv;
1162}
1163
1164static apr_status_t invalidate_entity(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r)
1165{
1166    /* mark the entity as invalidated */ view japanese
1167    h->cache_obj->info.control.invalidated = 1;
1168
1169    return commit_entity(h, r);
1170}
1171
1172static void *create_dir_config(apr_pool_t *p, char *dummy)
1173{
1174    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf =
1175            apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(cache_socache_dir_conf));
1176
1177    dconf->max = DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_SIZE;
1178    dconf->maxtime = apr_time_from_sec(DEFAULT_MAXTIME);
1179    dconf->mintime = apr_time_from_sec(DEFAULT_MINTIME);
1180    dconf->readsize = DEFAULT_READSIZE;
1181    dconf->readtime = DEFAULT_READTIME;
1182
1183    return dconf;
1184}
1185
1186static void *merge_dir_config(apr_pool_t *p, void *basev, void *addv)
1187{
1188    cache_socache_dir_conf
1189            *new =
1190                    (cache_socache_dir_conf *) apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(cache_socache_dir_conf));
1191    cache_socache_dir_conf *add = (cache_socache_dir_conf *) addv;
1192    cache_socache_dir_conf *base = (cache_socache_dir_conf *) basev;
1193
1194    new->max = (add->max_set == 0) ? base->max : add->max;
1195    new->max_set = add->max_set || base->max_set;
1196    new->maxtime = (add->maxtime_set == 0) ? base->maxtime : add->maxtime;
1197    new->maxtime_set = add->maxtime_set || base->maxtime_set;
1198    new->mintime = (add->mintime_set == 0) ? base->mintime : add->mintime;
1199    new->mintime_set = add->mintime_set || base->mintime_set;
1200    new->readsize = (add->readsize_set == 0) ? base->readsize : add->readsize;
1201    new->readsize_set = add->readsize_set || base->readsize_set;
1202    new->readtime = (add->readtime_set == 0) ? base->readtime : add->readtime;
1203    new->readtime_set = add->readtime_set || base->readtime_set;
1204
1205    return new;
1206}
1207
1208static void *create_config(apr_pool_t *p, server_rec *s)
1209{
1210    cache_socache_conf *conf = apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(cache_socache_conf));
1211
1212    return conf;
1213}
1214
1215static void *merge_config(apr_pool_t *p, void *basev, void *overridesv)
1216{
1217    cache_socache_conf *ps;
1218    cache_socache_conf *base = (cache_socache_conf *) basev;
1219    cache_socache_conf *overrides = (cache_socache_conf *) overridesv;
1220
1221    /* socache server config only has one field */ view japanese
1222    ps = overrides ? overrides : base;
1223
1224    return ps;
1225}
1226
1227/*
1228 * mod_cache_socache configuration directives handlers.
1229 */ view japanese
1230static const char *set_cache_socache(cmd_parms *cmd, void *in_struct_ptr,
1231        const char *arg)
1232{
1233    cache_socache_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(cmd->server->module_config,
1234            &cache_socache_module);
1235    cache_socache_provider_conf *provider = conf->provider
1236            = apr_pcalloc(cmd->pool, sizeof(cache_socache_provider_conf));
1237
1238    const char *err = NULL, *sep, *name;
1239
1240    /* Argument is of form 'name:args' or just 'name'. */ view japanese
1241    sep = ap_strchr_c(arg, ':');
1242    if (sep) {
1243        name = apr_pstrmemdup(cmd->pool, arg, sep - arg);
1244        sep++;
1245        provider->args = sep;
1246    }
1247    else {
1248        name = arg;
1249    }
1250
1251    provider->socache_provider = ap_lookup_provider(AP_SOCACHE_PROVIDER_GROUP,
1252            name, AP_SOCACHE_PROVIDER_VERSION);
1253    if (provider->socache_provider == NULL) {
1254        err = apr_psprintf(cmd->pool,
1255                    "Unknown socache provider '%s'. Maybe you need "
1256                    "to load the appropriate socache module "
1257                    "(mod_socache_%s?)", name, name);
1258    }
1259    return err;
1260}
1261
1262static const char *set_cache_max(cmd_parms *parms, void *in_struct_ptr,
1263        const char *arg)
1264{
1265    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf = (cache_socache_dir_conf *) in_struct_ptr;
1266
1267    if (apr_strtoff(&dconf->max, arg, NULL, 10) != APR_SUCCESS
1268            || dconf->max < 1024 || dconf->max > APR_UINT32_MAX) {
1269        return "CacheSocacheMaxSize argument must be a integer representing "
1270               "the max size of a cached entry (headers and body), at least 1024 "
1271               "and at most " APR_STRINGIFY(APR_UINT32_MAX);
1272    }
1273    dconf->max_set = 1;
1274    return NULL;
1275}
1276
1277static const char *set_cache_maxtime(cmd_parms *parms, void *in_struct_ptr,
1278        const char *arg)
1279{
1280    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf = (cache_socache_dir_conf *) in_struct_ptr;
1281    apr_off_t seconds;
1282
1283    if (apr_strtoff(&seconds, arg, NULL, 10) != APR_SUCCESS || seconds < 0) {
1284        return "CacheSocacheMaxTime argument must be the maximum amount of time in seconds to cache an entry.";
1285    }
1286    dconf->maxtime = apr_time_from_sec(seconds);
1287    dconf->maxtime_set = 1;
1288    return NULL;
1289}
1290
1291static const char *set_cache_mintime(cmd_parms *parms, void *in_struct_ptr,
1292        const char *arg)
1293{
1294    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf = (cache_socache_dir_conf *) in_struct_ptr;
1295    apr_off_t seconds;
1296
1297    if (apr_strtoff(&seconds, arg, NULL, 10) != APR_SUCCESS || seconds < 0) {
1298        return "CacheSocacheMinTime argument must be the minimum amount of time in seconds to cache an entry.";
1299    }
1300    dconf->mintime = apr_time_from_sec(seconds);
1301    dconf->mintime_set = 1;
1302    return NULL;
1303}
1304
1305static const char *set_cache_readsize(cmd_parms *parms, void *in_struct_ptr,
1306        const char *arg)
1307{
1308    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf = (cache_socache_dir_conf *) in_struct_ptr;
1309
1310    if (apr_strtoff(&dconf->readsize, arg, NULL, 10) != APR_SUCCESS
1311            || dconf->readsize < 0) {
1312        return "CacheSocacheReadSize argument must be a non-negative integer representing the max amount of data to cache in go.";
1313    }
1314    dconf->readsize_set = 1;
1315    return NULL;
1316}
1317
1318static const char *set_cache_readtime(cmd_parms *parms, void *in_struct_ptr,
1319        const char *arg)
1320{
1321    cache_socache_dir_conf *dconf = (cache_socache_dir_conf *) in_struct_ptr;
1322    apr_off_t milliseconds;
1323
1324    if (apr_strtoff(&milliseconds, arg, NULL, 10) != APR_SUCCESS
1325            || milliseconds < 0) {
1326        return "CacheSocacheReadTime argument must be a non-negative integer representing the max amount of time taken to cache in go.";
1327    }
1328    dconf->readtime = apr_time_from_msec(milliseconds);
1329    dconf->readtime_set = 1;
1330    return NULL;
1331}
1332
1333static apr_status_t remove_lock(void *data)
1334{
1335    if (socache_mutex) {
1336        apr_global_mutex_destroy(socache_mutex);
1337        socache_mutex = NULL;
1338    }
1339    return APR_SUCCESS;
1340}
1341
1342static apr_status_t destroy_cache(void *data)
1343{
1344    server_rec *s = data;
1345    cache_socache_conf *conf =
1346            ap_get_module_config(s->module_config, &cache_socache_module);
1347    if (conf->provider && conf->provider->socache_instance) {
1348        conf->provider->socache_provider->destroy(
1349                conf->provider->socache_instance, s);
1350        conf->provider->socache_instance = NULL;
1351    }
1352    return APR_SUCCESS;
1353}
1354
1355static int socache_status_hook(request_rec *r, int flags)
1356{
1357    apr_status_t status = APR_SUCCESS;
1358    cache_socache_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
1359                                                    &cache_socache_module);
1360    if (!conf->provider || !conf->provider->socache_provider ||
1361        !conf->provider->socache_instance) {
1362        return DECLINED;
1363    }
1364
1365    if (!(flags & AP_STATUS_SHORT)) {
1366        ap_rputs("<hr>\n"
1367                 "<table cellspacing=0 cellpadding=0>\n"
1368                 "<tr><td bgcolor=\"#000000\">\n"
1369                 "<b><font color=\"#ffffff\" face=\"Arial,Helvetica\">"
1370                 "mod_cache_socache Status:</font></b>\n"
1371                 "</td></tr>\n"
1372                 "<tr><td bgcolor=\"#ffffff\">\n", r);
1373    }
1374    else {
1375        ap_rputs("ModCacheSocacheStatus\n", r);
1376    }
1377
1378    if (socache_mutex) {
1379        status = apr_global_mutex_lock(socache_mutex);
1380        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
1381            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02816)
1382                    "could not acquire lock for cache status");
1383        }
1384    }
1385
1386    if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
1387        if (!(flags & AP_STATUS_SHORT)) {
1388            ap_rputs("No cache status data available\n", r);
1389        }
1390        else {
1391            ap_rputs("NotAvailable\n", r);
1392        }
1393    } else {
1394        conf->provider->socache_provider->status(conf->provider->socache_instance,
1395                                                 r, flags);
1396    }
1397
1398    if (socache_mutex && status == APR_SUCCESS) {
1399        status = apr_global_mutex_unlock(socache_mutex);
1400        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
1401            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02817)
1402                    "could not release lock for cache status");
1403        }
1404    }
1405
1406    if (!(flags & AP_STATUS_SHORT)) {
1407        ap_rputs("</td></tr>\n</table>\n", r);
1408    }
1409    return OK;
1410}
1411
1412static void socache_status_register(apr_pool_t *p)
1413{
1414    APR_OPTIONAL_HOOK(ap, status_hook, socache_status_hook, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
1415}
1416
1417static int socache_precfg(apr_pool_t *pconf, apr_pool_t *plog, apr_pool_t *ptmp)
1418{
1419    apr_status_t rv = ap_mutex_register(pconf, cache_socache_id, NULL,
1420            APR_LOCK_DEFAULT, 0);
1421    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1422        ap_log_perror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, plog, APLOGNO(02390)
1423                "failed to register %s mutex", cache_socache_id);
1424        return 500; /* An HTTP status would be a misnomer! */
1425    }
1426
1427    /* Register to handle mod_status status page generation */ view japanese
1428    socache_status_register(pconf);
1429
1430    return OK;
1431}
1432
1433static int socache_post_config(apr_pool_t *pconf, apr_pool_t *plog,
1434        apr_pool_t *ptmp, server_rec *base_server)
1435{
1436    server_rec *s;
1437    apr_status_t rv;
1438    const char *errmsg;
1439    static struct ap_socache_hints socache_hints =
1440    { 64, 2048, 60000000 };
1441
1442    for (s = base_server; s; s = s->next) {
1443        cache_socache_conf *conf =
1444                ap_get_module_config(s->module_config, &cache_socache_module);
1445
1446        if (!conf->provider) {
1447            continue;
1448        }
1449
1450        if (!socache_mutex && conf->provider->socache_provider->flags
1451                & AP_SOCACHE_FLAG_NOTMPSAFE) {
1452
1453            rv = ap_global_mutex_create(&socache_mutex, NULL, cache_socache_id,
1454                    NULL, s, pconf, 0);
1455            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1456                ap_log_perror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, plog, APLOGNO(02391)
1457                        "failed to create %s mutex", cache_socache_id);
1458                return 500; /* An HTTP status would be a misnomer! */
1459            }
1460            apr_pool_cleanup_register(pconf, NULL, remove_lock,
1461                    apr_pool_cleanup_null);
1462        }
1463
1464        errmsg = conf->provider->socache_provider->create(
1465                &conf->provider->socache_instance, conf->provider->args, ptmp,
1466                pconf);
1467        if (errmsg) {
1468            ap_log_perror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, plog,
1469                    APLOGNO(02392) "%s", errmsg);
1470            return 500; /* An HTTP status would be a misnomer! */
1471        }
1472
1473        rv = conf->provider->socache_provider->init(
1474                conf->provider->socache_instance, cache_socache_id,
1475                &socache_hints, s, pconf);
1476        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1477            ap_log_perror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, plog, APLOGNO(02393)
1478                    "failed to initialise %s cache", cache_socache_id);
1479            return 500; /* An HTTP status would be a misnomer! */ view japanese
1480        }
1481        apr_pool_cleanup_register(pconf, (void *) s, destroy_cache,
1482                apr_pool_cleanup_null);
1483
1484    }
1485
1486    return OK;
1487}
1488
1489static void socache_child_init(apr_pool_t *p, server_rec *s)
1490{
1491    const char *lock;
1492    apr_status_t rv;
1493    if (!socache_mutex) {
1494        return; /* don't waste the overhead of creating mutex & cache */ view japanese
1495    }
1496    lock = apr_global_mutex_lockfile(socache_mutex);
1497    rv = apr_global_mutex_child_init(&socache_mutex, lock, p);
1498    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1499        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, s, APLOGNO(02394)
1500                "failed to initialise mutex in child_init");
1501    }
1502}
1503
1504static const command_rec cache_socache_cmds[] =
1505{
1506    AP_INIT_TAKE1("CacheSocache", set_cache_socache, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
1507            "The shared object cache to store cache files"),
1508    AP_INIT_TAKE1("CacheSocacheMaxTime", set_cache_maxtime, NULL, RSRC_CONF | ACCESS_CONF,
1509            "The maximum cache expiry age to cache a document in seconds"),
1510    AP_INIT_TAKE1("CacheSocacheMinTime", set_cache_mintime, NULL, RSRC_CONF | ACCESS_CONF,
1511            "The minimum cache expiry age to cache a document in seconds"),
1512    AP_INIT_TAKE1("CacheSocacheMaxSize", set_cache_max, NULL, RSRC_CONF | ACCESS_CONF,
1513            "The maximum cache entry size (headers and body) to cache a document"),
1514    AP_INIT_TAKE1("CacheSocacheReadSize", set_cache_readsize, NULL, RSRC_CONF | ACCESS_CONF,
1515            "The maximum quantity of data to attempt to read and cache in one go"),
1516    AP_INIT_TAKE1("CacheSocacheReadTime", set_cache_readtime, NULL, RSRC_CONF | ACCESS_CONF,
1517            "The maximum time taken to attempt to read and cache in go"),
1518    { NULL }
1519};
1520
1521static const cache_provider cache_socache_provider =
1522{
1523    &remove_entity, &store_headers, &store_body, &recall_headers, &recall_body,
1524    &create_entity, &open_entity, &remove_url, &commit_entity,
1525    &invalidate_entity
1526};
1527
1528static void cache_socache_register_hook(apr_pool_t *p)
1529{
1530    /* cache initializer */ view japanese
1531    ap_register_provider(p, CACHE_PROVIDER_GROUP, "socache", "0",
1532            &cache_socache_provider);
1533    ap_hook_pre_config(socache_precfg, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
1534    ap_hook_post_config(socache_post_config, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
1535    ap_hook_child_init(socache_child_init, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
1536}
1537
1538AP_DECLARE_MODULE(cache_socache) = { STANDARD20_MODULE_STUFF,
1539    create_dir_config,  /* create per-directory config structure */ view japanese
1540    merge_dir_config, /* merge per-directory config structures */ view japanese
1541    create_config, /* create per-server config structure */ view japanese
1542    merge_config, /* merge per-server config structures */ view japanese
1543    cache_socache_cmds, /* command apr_table_t */ view japanese
1544    cache_socache_register_hook /* register hooks */ view japanese
1545};

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:44-67

/*
 * mod_cache_socache: 共有オブジェクトキャッシュベースのHTTP 1.1キャッシュ。
 *
 * エントリ検索のフロー:
 *   クライアントからのURI /foo/bar/baz に対するリクエスト
 *   URIキーを取得 (フォーマット#1またはフォーマット#2が含まれる場合がある)
 *   フォーマット#1の場合 (Varyヘッダーのリストを含む):
 *      各ヘッダー名 (.header) をリクエスト値 (headers_in) と組み合わせて使用し、
 *      HeaderName+HeaderValue+.../foo/bar/baz を使用してキーを再生成する
 *      キーを再読み込みする (フォーマット#2である必要がある)
 *
 * フォーマット#1:
 *   apr_uint32_t format;
 *   apr_time_t expire;
 *   apr_array_t vary_headers (CRLF区切り)
 *
 * フォーマット#2:
 *   cache_socache_info_t (最初の sizeof(apr_uint32_t) バイトがフォーマット)
 *   エンティティ名 (sobj->name) [長さは cache_socache_info_t->name_len に格納]
 *   r->headers_out (CRLF区切り)
 *   CRLF
 *   r->headers_in (CRLF区切り)
 *   CRLF
 */

Original comment

/*
 * mod_cache_socache: Shared Object Cache Based HTTP 1.1 Cache.
 *
 * Flow to Find the entry:
 *   Incoming client requests URI /foo/bar/baz
 *   Fetch URI key (may contain Format #1 or Format #2)
 *   If format #1 (Contains a list of Vary Headers):
 *      Use each header name (from .header) with our request values (headers_in) to
 *      regenerate key using HeaderName+HeaderValue+.../foo/bar/baz
 *      re-read in key (must be format #2)
 *
 * Format #1:
 *   apr_uint32_t format;
 *   apr_time_t expire;
 *   apr_array_t vary_headers (delimited by CRLF)
 *
 * Format #2:
 *   cache_socache_info_t (first sizeof(apr_uint32_t) bytes is the format)
 *   entity name (sobj->name) [length is in cache_socache_info_t->name_len]
 *   r->headers_out (delimited by CRLF)
 *   CRLF
 *   r->headers_in (delimited by CRLF)
 *   CRLF
 */

Japanese translation

 * cache_socache_object_t
 * cache_object_t::vobj から参照される
 */

Original comment

 * cache_socache_object_t

 */

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:135-137

/*
 * ローカル静的関数
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Local static functions
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:222

/* apr_isspace('\r') は真であるため、上記からの \r を超えて進まないこと */

Original comment

/* Do not go past the \r from above as apr_isspace('\r') is true */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:293-315

/* TODO:
     *    - 複数の値を持つヘッダーの処理を改善する。（ソートするか？）
     *    - 大文字・小文字を区別しない値の処理を改善する。
     *        キャッシュヒット率が下がるだけなので致命的ではないが、修正できれば望ましい。
     *
     * 大半の値（エンコーディングなど）は、大文字・小文字を区別しない。
     * rfc2616 の大部分において、ヘッダーの内容は大文字・小文字を区別しない。
     *
     * したがって、より良い解決策は、大文字・小文字を区別して扱うべきヘッダーを特定することかもしれない。
     *  HTTP URI (3.2.3) [ホストとスキームは区別しない]
     *  HTTP メソッド (5.1.1)
     *  HTTP 日付値 (3.3.1)
     *  3.7 メディアタイプ [抜粋]
     *     タイプ、サブタイプ、およびパラメータ属性名は、大文字・小文字を区別しない。
     *     パラメータ値は、パラメータ名のセマンティクスに応じて、大文字・小文字を区別する場合としない場合がある。
     *  4.20 Except [抜粋]
     *     期待値の比較は、引用符で囲まれていないトークン（100-continue トークンを含む）については大文字・小文字を区別せず、
     *     引用符で囲まれた文字列の期待値拡張については大文字・小文字を区別する。
     */

Original comment

/* TODO:
     *    - Handle multiple-value headers better. (sort them?)
     *    - Handle Case in-sensitive Values better.
     *        This isn't the end of the world, since it just lowers the cache
     *        hit rate, but it would be nice to fix.
     *
     * The majority are case insenstive if they are values (encoding etc).
     * Most of rfc2616 is case insensitive on header contents.
     *
     * So the better solution may be to identify headers which should be
     * treated case-sensitive?
     *  HTTP URI's (3.2.3) [host and scheme are insensitive]
     *  HTTP method (5.1.1)
     *  HTTP-date values (3.3.1)
     *  3.7 Media Types [excerpt]
     *     The type, subtype, and parameter attribute names are case-
     *     insensitive. Parameter values might or might not be case-sensitive,
     *     depending on the semantics of the parameter name.
     *  4.20 Except [excerpt]
     *     Comparison of expectation values is case-insensitive for unquoted
     *     tokens (including the 100-continue token), and is case-sensitive for
     *     quoted-string expectation-extensions.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:350

/* "Vary: A, B" と "Vary: B, A" が同じように格納されるようソートする。 */

Original comment

/* Sort it so that "Vary: A, B" and "Vary: B, A" are stored the same. */

Japanese translation

 */

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* TODO: but we could */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:381-393

/*
     * 鶏と卵の問題がある。store_headers を試みるまで、レスポンスがどれほどの大きさになるか、
     * また設定されたキャッシュ制限に収まるかどうかは分からない。しかし、試みるかどうかを
     * 今すぐ決定する必要がある。もし誤った判断を下せば、cache_disk のような他のキャッシュ
     * 実装がキャッシュする機会を奪うことになり、それは望ましくない。
     *
     * 低コストなものから高コストなものへと一連のテストを行い、念のため小さなマージンを
     * 設けた上で、今回のキャッシュ試行を無視するかどうかを決定する。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * We have a chicken and egg problem. We don't know until we
     * attempt to store_headers just how big the response will be
     * and whether it will fit in the cache limits set. But we
     * need to make a decision now as to whether we plan to try.
     * If we make the wrong decision, we could prevent another
     * cache implementation, such as cache_disk, from getting the
     * opportunity to cache, and that would be unfortunate.
     *
     * In a series of tests, from cheapest to most expensive,
     * decide whether or not to ignore this attempt to cache,
     * with a small margin just to be sure.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:407

/* 現在のヘッダーに基づき、キャッシュされた合計サイズを見積もる */

Original comment

/* estimate the total cached size, given current headers */

/* バッファ用の一時プールを作成します。何らかのエラーが発生した場合は、
     * レスポンスの存続期間中に未使用のメモリが大きなバッファとして残らないよう、
     * プールを破棄します。
     */

Original comment

/* Create a temporary pool for the buffer, and destroy it if something
     * goes wrong so we don't have large buffers of unused memory hanging
     * about for the lifetime of the response.
     */

Japanese translation

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:669-678

/* recall_body() によって出力フィルターのブリゲードに後で連結されるボディブリゲードを作成する。
         * sobj->buffer (データ) は sobj->pool (r->pool のサブプール) を指しているため、
         * バケットが生存している間に sobj->pool が破棄された場合にヒープへ変化できる
         * プールバケットを使用して安全を確保する。しかし、バケットがまだ sobj->body に
         * 存在する間に sobj->pool が破棄された場合 (つまり recall_body() が一度も
         * 呼び出されなかった場合)、解放される直前のものに変化させる必要はない。
         * そのため、これが発生する前に pre_cleanup が sobj->body のクリーンアップを
         * 行う (それ以外の場合は何もしない)。
         */

Original comment

/* Create the body brigade later concatenated to the output filters'
         * brigade by recall_body(). Since sobj->buffer (the data) point to
         * sobj->pool (a subpool of r->pool), be safe by using a pool bucket
         * which can morph to heap if sobj->pool is destroyed while the bucket
         * is still alive. But if sobj->pool gets destroyed while the bucket is
         * still in sobj->body (i.e. recall_body() was never called), we don't
         * need to morph to something just about to be freed, so a pre_cleanup
         * will take care of cleaning up sobj->body before this happens (and is
         * a noop otherwise).
         */

Japanese translation

/* Remove the intermediate cache file and return non-APR_SUCCESS */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:1018

/* 空のバケットをキャッシュに書き込まない */

Original comment

/* don't write empty buckets to the cache */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:1037-1041

/* 一度に書き込む準備ができている上限に達しましたか？
         * もしそうなら、終了します。後で再度呼び出されます。これは、一度に大量のデータを
         * 処理しようとしたり、データの書き込みに時間がかかりすぎてクライアントが
         * タイムアウトしたりするのを防ぐためです。
         */

Original comment

/* have we reached the limit of how much we're prepared to write in one
         * go? If so, leave, we'll get called again. This prevents us from trying
         * to swallow too much data at once, or taking so long to write the data
         * the client times out.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:1054-1055

/* これが最後のバケットか？もしそうなら、健全性チェックを実行する。
     */

Original comment

/* Was this the final bucket? If yes, perform sanity checks.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:1081

/* すべてのチェックは正常です。コミットが来れば実行可能です */

Original comment

/* All checks were fine, we're good to go when the commit comes */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_cache_socache.c:1136-1138

/* 安全のため、再検証に成功しなかった場合に備えて、既存のエントリがあれば削除する。
     */

Original comment

/* For safety, remove any existing entry on failure, just in case it could not
     * be revalidated successfully.
     */
